setup_task.sh creates file at this exact path
ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) is a business logistics system per Purdue Enterprise Reference Architecture Level 4 specification
Email servers are corporate IT services at Level 4 per Purdue model
Jump hosts are DMZ components (Level 3.5) serving as buffer zones between IT and OT per industrial security standards
Plant historians are manufacturing operations systems at Level 3 per Purdue Enterprise Reference Architecture specification
Manufacturing Execution Systems (MES) are manufacturing operations systems at Level 3 per Purdue model
Human Machine Interfaces (HMI) are supervisory control systems at Level 2 per Purdue Enterprise Reference Architecture
Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) systems are supervisory control systems at Level 2 per Purdue model
Programmable Logic Controllers (PLCs) are intelligent devices at Level 1 per Purdue Enterprise Reference Architecture
Robot controllers are intelligent devices that manipulate physical processes at Level 1 per Purdue model
Variable Frequency Drives (VFDs) are intelligent devices for field control at Level 1 per Purdue Enterprise Reference Architecture

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims are verified. The task uses synthetic data created by setup_task.sh. The file paths for the diagram (/home/ga/Diagrams/factory_audit.drawio), inventory (/home/ga/Desktop/device_inventory.txt), and PDF export (/home/ga/Diagrams/exports/purdue_network.pdf) are exactly as specified. The ground-truth device classifications follow the Purdue Enterprise Reference Architecture (PERA) standard: ERP and Email are Level 4 business systems; Jump Host is Level 3.5 DMZ; Historian and MES are Level 3 manufacturing operations; HMI and SCADA are Level 2 supervisory controls; PLC, Robot, and VFD are Level 1 intelligent devices. All classifications are consistent with PERA specifications.
